items.headline: NSA Project Aether (Bloomberg Apr 30)
items.diff_narrative: **New: NSA Project Aether (Bloomberg Apr 30) — NSA benchmarks Anthropic Mythos Preview against sovereign classified cyber tools targeting Microsoft software vulnerabilities; Pentagon CTO May 1 statement separates Anthropic-as-supply-chain-risk blacklist from Mythos engagement; Mythos Preview = Apr 7 release under Project Glasswing with ~50 launch partners (AWS, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, JPMorgan, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Palo Alto Networks + ~40 critical-software-infrastructure orgs); $100M usage credits + $4M open-source security donations** — Yesterday's report missed the Mythos / Glasswing context entirely. Today re-frames the May 1 Pentagon-Anthropic stand-off: the freeze is procurement-stack-level, but the same model the Pentagon refused to procure is the one the NSA depends on for sovereign cyber benchmarking. Structurally re-reads the May 1 "frontier-lab × DoD line in the sand" framing as more nuanced.

items.headline: Pentagon May 1 vendor count is 8, not 7
items.diff_narrative: **Correction: Pentagon May 1 vendor count is 8, not 7 — Oracle was added within hours of the original 7-vendor announcement (SpaceX, OpenAI, Google, NVIDIA, Reflection, Microsoft, AWS + Oracle); Anthropic still explicitly excluded; existing Anthropic deployments in classified networks given a 6-month sunset window** — Yesterday's report identified the 7-vendor split. Today corrects the count and adds the existing-Anthropic-deployment 6-month sunset framing.

items.headline: Microsoft Agent 365 Registry Sync (May 1)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Microsoft Agent 365 Registry Sync (May 1) — alongside the GA, Microsoft ships public preview of cross-cloud Agent Registry Sync with AWS Bedrock + Google Cloud Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform; Defender per-agent relationship-map surfaces MCP servers + identities + cloud resources as a single ops view; Anthropic Claude Code + GitHub Copilot CLI named as upcoming local-agent integrations after the open-source OpenClaw entry path; MCP SDK monthly downloads ~97M in March 2026** — Yesterday's report had the Agent 365 GA but missed the Registry Sync multicloud preview, which is the structural-precedent-shift for the MCP-Gateway thesis (single-vendor → cross-cloud protocol).

items.headline: Anthropic creative connectors (Apr 28 carry into the May 2-3 weekend)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Anthropic creative connectors (Apr 28 carry into the May 2-3 weekend) — 9 Claude connectors plug Anthropic into Adobe Creative Cloud, Blender, Ableton Live, Autodesk Fusion, Splice, SketchUp, Affinity by Canva, Resolume Arena, Resolume Wire; Anthropic becomes a Corporate Patron of the Blender Development Fund (joining Netflix, Epic, Wacom)** — Yesterday's report did not flag the creative-connector wave. Today adds it as Anthropic's white-space pivot while Pentagon-locked-out: cyber (Mythos) + creative (connectors) become the operative go-to-market vectors during the procurement freeze.

items.headline: Tesla Optimus Q2 2026 Fremont production prep
items.diff_narrative: **New: Tesla Optimus Q2 2026 Fremont production prep — Tesla starts the first large-scale Optimus humanoid-robot factory build-out at Fremont in Q2, phasing out legacy Model S + Model X production lines to make room for a first-generation 1M-units/year nameplate robotics plant; Optimus 3 launches "summer 2026" with hundreds of units this year ramping to thousands then tens of thousands by 2027-2028; price target $20K-$30K** — Yesterday's report had Apptronik + Figure + Tesla Optimus Gen 3 carry but not the Fremont 1M-units/year nameplate facility plan. Today adds it as the first hyperscaler-style physical-AI capex commitment.

items.headline: Iran-tension oil spike
items.diff_narrative: **New: Iran-tension oil spike — WTI crude near $105 (+12% on the week) as the May 1 State Department + Treasury joint Iran enforcement package + standing 25% Trump secondary-tariff threat reprices Mideast risk into the energy bid; CBOE VIX 16.99 (+0.59%) signals contained equity volatility into the May 1 record-tape weekend** — Yesterday's report had the May 1 Iran enforcement package but did not quantify the WTI move. Today adds the energy-bid leg as the swing macro variable into May 5 AMD + May 8 Apr Jobs week.

items.headline: Anthropic Mythos Preview model card (April 7 launch + AWS Bedrock gated research preview)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Anthropic Mythos Preview model card (April 7 launch + AWS Bedrock gated research preview) — Anthropic restricts Mythos to Project Glasswing partners only; AWS Bedrock ships Mythos as a Gated Research Preview SKU; red.anthropic.com/2026/mythos-preview/ posted as the technical disclosure page; Schneier April writeup confirms the cyber-capability framing; Anthropic publicly states "Mythos is currently far ahead of any other AI model in cyber capabilities"** — Yesterday's report didn't contain Mythos at all. Today carries the full disclosure stack as the foundation for the new gated-frontier-cyber-AI thesis.

items.headline: AMD May 5 Q1 2026 print preview
items.diff_narrative: **New: AMD May 5 Q1 2026 print preview — consensus EPS $1.28 / rev $9.88B (+33% YoY); guidance $9.8B (+/- $300M); gross margin -2pp QoQ to 55% (vs Q4 2025's $360M one-time MI308 China inventory-reserve reversal); MI400 ramp pace + OpenAI 6GW first-deployment H2 2026 + MI450 capacity planning + customer diversification are the focal investor questions; DA Davidson Buy at $375 PT (raised from $220 after Intel Q1 print)** — Yesterday's report had the AMD May 5 print and the DA Davidson Buy upgrade. Today adds the gross-margin-compression detail + the OpenAI 6GW H2 first-deployment timing + the MI450 capacity question.

items.headline: Apr Jobs Report scheduled May 8 8:30 AM ET (BLS confirmed)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Apr Jobs Report scheduled May 8 8:30 AM ET (BLS confirmed) — first major macro print after May 5 AMD; March baseline 178K + UE 4.3% (BLS Apr 3 release); structurally the first non-earnings macro tape into the May 15 Fed Chair handoff window** — Yesterday's report contained the Apr Jobs date correction (from May 1 to May 8) but didn't carry the March baseline or the structural framing into the Chair handoff.

items.headline: Google Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite
items.diff_narrative: **New: Google Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite — 2.5x faster response time, 45% faster output generation vs prior Gemini, $0.25 per million input tokens; positioned for high-volume agentic workloads against Anthropic Haiku 4.5 + OpenAI's small-model tier** — Yesterday's report did not flag the Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite tier. Today adds it as the Google-side per-token-margin signal mirroring Anthropic + DeepSeek + Qwen pricing-floor competition.

items.headline: arxiv KV-cache + agent-eval wave Apr 28 - May 3
items.diff_narrative: **New: arxiv KV-cache + agent-eval wave Apr 28 - May 3 — SideQuest (model-driven KV cache management for long-horizon agentic reasoning, arxiv 2602.22603 carry); DepthKV (layer-dependent KV cache pruning for long-context inference, 2604.24647); SAGE (service agent graph-guided eval benchmark, 2604.09285); LiveClawBench (LLM agents on real-world assistant tasks, 2604.13072); GAIA-v2-LILT (multilingual agent benchmark beyond translation, 2604.24929); KVFundaBench (comprehensive KV cache compression benchmark across world knowledge / reasoning / code / safety / long-context)** — Yesterday's report had the WindowsWorld + BenchGuard + multi-agent-vuln-discovery + Agent Harness Survey + CodeComp / CacheFlow / TokenDance / TTKV / PolyKV wave. Today adds the SideQuest-style reasoning-driven KV-cache compression + DepthKV layer-pruning + SAGE/LiveClawBench/GAIA-v2-LILT eval wave.

items.headline: AI Tinkerers SF Generative UI Global Hackathon: Agentic Interfaces (May 9)
items.diff_narrative: **New: AI Tinkerers SF Generative UI Global Hackathon: Agentic Interfaces (May 9) — engineers build agentic UIs from model output, supported by Google DeepMind + CopilotKit; engineers build interactive UIs from model output using cutting-edge protocols, defining the generative UI stack through tool-enabled interface execution** — Yesterday's report had the May 9 hackathon but didn't surface the Google DeepMind + CopilotKit sponsorship or the "agentic interfaces from model output" framing.

items.headline: Microsoft Build 2026 detailed agenda (June 2-3 Fort Mason SF)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Microsoft Build 2026 detailed agenda (June 2-3 Fort Mason SF) — 8 distinct session formats with hands-on-labs + peer-collaboration emphasis; in-person attendance shrunk to ~2,500 (down from 5,000+ in prior years); Satya Nadella opening keynote on AI strategy + Azure roadmap + dev tooling; GitHub + Microsoft Foundry leadership presenting; in-person tickets $1,099; keynotes livestreamed free** — Yesterday's report had the Microsoft Build June 2-3 anchor + session catalog mention. Today adds the format-shift detail (smaller, hands-on, fewer keynote slots).

items.headline: Simon Willison May 2 weblog "Sightings"
items.diff_narrative: **New: Simon Willison May 2 weblog "Sightings" — small JS app fetching iNaturalist data for personal homepage syndication, built on phone with Claude Code for web; backfills decade of iNaturalist observations into site search + date archive pages** — Yesterday's report had Willison May 1 Codex CLI 0.128.0 changelog. Today adds the May 2 Sightings entry as further "personal AI as headless tooling" data-point — same dormant-pool 'Headless Everything' adjacency.

items.headline: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8)
items.diff_narrative: **Continuing: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) — still unpatched into May 3 (~14 days post-disclosure); CERT/CC mitigation guidance recommends ImmutableSandboxedEnvironment substitution as the workaround until upstream fix lands; LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 (CVSS 9.3) carry; Spring AI CVE-2026-40967 (CVSS 8.6) carry; UTT HiPER 1200GW + IBM Langflow CVE-2026-6543 (May 1 wave) carry; cPanel & WHM CVE-2026-41940 KEV (May 21 federal deadline) carry; Linux Copy Fail CVE-2026-31431 KEV (May 15 federal deadline) carry** — Carry-forward; SGLang now the longest-running 2026 unpatched LLM-tooling CVE at 14 days.

items.headline: Mag-7 super-week + AAPL Q3 guide +14-17% + $100B buyback + $700B 2026 capex tape + Cerebras IPO mid-May + Anthropic Opus 4.7 across Claude products + API + Bedrock + Vertex AI + Microsoft Foundry + AWS Bedrock AgentCore Runtime Node.js + Codex CLI 0.128.0 + Microsoft Foundry Agent Framework 1.0 GA + OpenAI Agents SDK + Powell stays past May 15 + Warsh Senate Banking confirm + Apr 28 ATT&CK v19 named techniques + CSAI Foundation MITRE-CNA + NIST CAISI agentic-AI security RFI
items.diff_narrative: **Continuing: Mag-7 super-week + AAPL Q3 guide +14-17% + $100B buyback + $700B 2026 capex tape + Cerebras IPO mid-May + Anthropic Opus 4.7 across Claude products + API + Bedrock + Vertex AI + Microsoft Foundry + AWS Bedrock AgentCore Runtime Node.js + Codex CLI 0.128.0 + Microsoft Foundry Agent Framework 1.0 GA + OpenAI Agents SDK + Powell stays past May 15 + Warsh Senate Banking confirm + Apr 28 ATT&CK v19 named techniques + CSAI Foundation MITRE-CNA + NIST CAISI agentic-AI security RFI** — Carry-forward.

items.diff_narrative: **Continuing: DeepSeek V4 Pro / V4 Flash + Qwen 3.6 + Kimi K2.6 + GLM-5.1 (MIT license) + PrismML Bonsai-8B 1-bit + Foundry Local GA + Unsloth 2026.4.8 + TurboQuant + PolyKV + NVIDIA kvpress + SGLang TurboQuant #21419 + #23134 + llama.cpp #20969 + new arxiv wave (CodeComp / CacheFlow / TokenDance / TTKV)** — Carry-forward.
